name: Conduct Search Engine Discovery Reconnaissance
description: Search engine discovery helps identify information that may have been inadvertently exposed, including sensitive data, internal network information, or development artifacts.
objectives: Identify sensitive information exposed through search engines
Find exposed configuration files or backup files
test_cases: Use Google dorks: site:target.com filetype:pdf
Search for: site:target.com ext:xml OR ext:conf OR ext:cnf OR ext:reg OR ext:inf OR ext:rdp OR ext:cfg OR ext:txt OR ext:ora OR ext:ini
Use Shodan: hostname:target.com
Search for exposed .git directories: site:target.com '.git'
Use theHarvester: theHarvester -d target.com -b google,bing,linkedin
remediation: Implement robots.txt properly, use meta noindex tags for sensitive pages, and regularly audit public information exposure.

name: Fingerprint Web Server
description: Determine the web server type, version, and configuration to identify known vulnerabilities.
objectives: Determine web server type and version
test_cases: Review HTTP response headers: curl -I https://target.com
Use Netcraft for server identification
Run whatweb: whatweb -a 3 https://target.com
Use Nmap service detection: nmap -sV -p 80,443 target.com
Check error pages for server information disclosure
remediation: Remove server version headers (Server, X-Powered-By). Customize error pages to not disclose server information.

name: Test Network Infrastructure Configuration
description: Assess the configuration of network infrastructure for security weaknesses.
test_cases: Port scan: nmap -sV -p- --open target.com
Test SSL/TLS: testssl.sh https://target.com
Check for default credentials on network devices
Test for DNS zone transfer: dig axfr target.com @ns1.target.com
Verify HTTPS enforcement and HSTS header
remediation: Disable unnecessary services. Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Use strong TLS configurations (TLS 1.2+).

name: Testing for Credentials Transported over an Encrypted Channel
description: Verify that credentials are transmitted securely over encrypted channels.
objectives: Verify credentials are not sent over HTTP
test_cases: Intercept login form submission with Burp Suite and verify HTTPS
Check if login page loads over HTTP: curl -v http://target.com/login
Verify Set-Cookie headers include Secure and HttpOnly flags
Test for forced HTTP to HTTPS redirect
Check for HSTS header: curl -I https://target.com | grep -i strict
remediation: Enforce HTTPS for all pages. Set Secure flag on session cookies. Implement HSTS.

name: Testing for Default Credentials
description: Test whether the application uses default credentials for authentication.
objectives: Identify use of default usernames and passwords
Check for known default credentials in admin interfaces
test_cases: Try common default credentials: admin/admin, admin/password, admin/123456
Check for admin panels: /admin, /manager, /administrator, /wp-admin
Use Metasploit auxiliary modules for default credential scanning
Review vendor documentation for default credentials
Run: hydra -L /usr/share/wordlists/default_users.txt -P /usr/share/wordlists/default_passwords.txt target.com http-get /admin
remediation: Change all default credentials during deployment. Implement account lockout policies.

name: Testing Directory Traversal File Include
description: Test for path traversal vulnerabilities that allow access to files outside the intended directory.
objectives: Attempt to read sensitive system files
test_cases: Test URL parameters: ?file=../../../../etc/passwd
Try URL encoding: ?file=..%2F..%2F..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd
Test double encoding: ?file=..%252F..%252F..%252Fetc%252Fpasswd
Use DotDotPwn: dotdotpwn -m http -h target.com -u 'http://target.com/page?file=TRAVERSAL'
Test Windows paths: ?file=..\..\..\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts
remediation: Validate and sanitize all file path inputs. Use a whitelist of allowed files. Avoid passing user-supplied data to file system APIs.

name: Testing for Reflected Cross Site Scripting
description: Identify and test for reflected XSS vulnerabilities where user input is immediately returned by the web application.
objectives: Identify input vectors that are reflected in the response
Determine if input is properly sanitized
test_cases: Test all input fields with: <script>alert(1)</script>
Try HTML encoding bypass: <script>alert(1)</script>
Test event handlers: " onmouseover="alert(1)
Use XSS Cheat Sheet for advanced payloads
Automated scan: xssstrike --url 'http://target.com/search?q=test' --crawl
remediation: Implement output encoding. Use Content Security Policy. Validate and sanitize all input.

name: Testing for SQL Injection
description: Test for SQL injection vulnerabilities that allow manipulation of database queries.
objectives: Attempt data extraction or authentication bypass
test_cases: Test with single quote: ' OR '1'='1
Try time-based blind: ' OR SLEEP(5)--
Use sqlmap: sqlmap -u 'http://target.com/page?id=1' --dbs
Test login bypass: admin'-- in username field
Test with UNION: ' UNION SELECT NULL,NULL,NULL--
remediation: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements. Implement input validation. Apply least privilege to database accounts.

name: Testing for Code Injection
description: Test for code injection vulnerabilities that allow execution of attacker-controlled code on the server.
objectives: Determine if user input is evaluated as code
test_cases: Test PHP injection: ;phpinfo();
Test Python: import os;os.system('id')
Test template injection: {{7*7}} (Jinja2), ${7*7} (Freemarker)
Use tplmap for template injection: tplmap -u 'http://target.com/page?name=test'
Test for command injection: ; id, | id, && id
remediation: Never pass user input to eval() or similar functions. Use sandboxed template engines. Validate all input.

name: Testing for Session Management Schema
description: Analyze the session management mechanism to identify weaknesses in session token generation and handling.
objectives: Analyze session token entropy and randomness
test_cases: Collect multiple session tokens and analyze for patterns
Test session token entropy with Burp Sequencer
Verify session is invalidated after logout
Test concurrent session handling
Check cookie attributes: Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite
remediation: Use cryptographically secure random session tokens. Implement proper session expiration. Set secure cookie attributes.

name: Testing for Cross Site Request Forgery
description: Test for CSRF vulnerabilities that allow attackers to perform actions on behalf of authenticated users.
objectives: Identify state-changing requests without CSRF protection
Test for CSRF token bypass techniques
test_cases: Identify all state-changing requests (POST, PUT, DELETE)
Check for CSRF tokens in forms and AJAX requests
Test if CSRF token is validated server-side
Try removing or modifying the CSRF token
Test SameSite cookie attribute bypass for cross-origin requests
remediation: Implement synchronizer token pattern. Use SameSite cookie attribute. Verify Origin and Referer headers.

name: Testing for Weak Transport Layer Security
description: Test the SSL/TLS configuration for known weaknesses and insecure configurations.
test_cases: Run testssl.sh: testssl.sh --full https://target.com
Check for SSLv2/SSLv3/TLS 1.0/1.1 support
Test for BEAST, POODLE, HEARTBLEED vulnerabilities
Verify certificate chain and expiration
Check for HSTS with long max-age: curl -I https://target.com | grep -i strict
remediation: Disable SSLv2, SSLv3, TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1. Use only strong cipher suites. Implement HSTS.
